scope: EU member-state family-law practitioners (DE/FR/ES/IT/NL/BE) who have demonstrably handled parental-alienation cases at apex level, published in the field, chaired an official PA-related expert body, or publicly stated a sourced stance.

stance_taxonomy.recognition: Treats PA / alienating behaviour as a legitimate, litigable phenomenon
stance_taxonomy.critique: Argues PA / SAP / alienazione genitoriale is misused in family courts, especially against DV survivors
stance_taxonomy.middle: Procedural-reform / differentiated approach engaging both DV and alienating-behaviour frames
stance_taxonomy.not_publicly_confirmed: Included for record-completeness; PA-specific stance not in primary sources

entries.name: Kerstin Bielefeld
named_cases_or_publications: BVerfG 1 BvR 1076/23, Beschluss vom 17.11.2023 — Bevollmaechtigte (counsel of record) for the constitutional complainant; BVerfG annulled OLG Köln 25 UF 19/23 transfer-of-residence order based on Eltern-Kind-Entfremdung framing
entries.hook: Only attorney in 2026 Germany with a published BVerfG win that explicitly classifies PAS as pseudoscientific — anchor for any DE constitutional-litigation outreach.

entries.name: Eva Becker
named_cases_or_publications: Chair, DAV-Familienrechtsausschuss
DAV-AG Familienrecht FamR series: FamR 01/23, 12/24, 23/24, 25/24, 3/26 on PAS / Eltern-Kind-Entfremdung jurisprudence
entries.hook: Chairs the standing committee that signs the German Bar's PA / Eltern-Kind-Entfremdung policy positions — single highest-leverage institutional contact in DE.

entries.name: Jochem Schausten
entries.credentials: Rechtsanwalt; Vorsitzender Arbeitsgemeinschaft Familienrecht im DAV
entries.address: DAV AG Familienrecht via DAV-Hauptgeschäftsstelle, Littenstr. 11, 10179 Berlin (institutional)
named_cases_or_publications: Chair, AG Familienrecht im DAV (largest DAV working group, ~4,788 members July 2025)
Institutional editorial line on FamR newsroom series
entries.hook: Speaks for ~4,800 German family-law specialists — natural press / professional-contact route for any DE PA-law commentary.

entries.name: SCP Waquet, Farge et Hazan
entries.credentials: Avocats au Conseil d'État et à la Cour de cassation (SCP)
entries.address: Paris, France (Ordre des avocats aux Conseils)
entries.bar: Ordre des avocats au Conseil d'État et à la Cour de cassation
named_cases_or_publications: Counsel of record in Cass. 1re civ., 26 juin 2013, n° 12-14.392 — first apex French SAP recognition arrêt
entries.hook: Historic French Cour de cassation anchor for SAP-recognition jurisprudence.

entries.name: SCP Richard (successor: SCP Boré, Salve de Bruneton et Mégret)
entries.credentials: Avocats au Conseil d'État et à la Cour de cassation (SCP)
entries.firm: Successor SCP Boré, Salve de Bruneton et Mégret
entries.bar: Ordre des avocats au Conseil d'État et à la Cour de cassation
named_cases_or_publications: Opposing counsel in Cass. 1re civ., 26 juin 2013, n° 12-14.392
entries.hook: Historic litigant on the FR PA-recognition arrêt; record completeness only.

entries.name: Michèle Bauer
entries.credentials: Maître; Avocate au Barreau de Bordeaux (admise 2003); CNB spécialiste droit social
entries.address: 33 Cours Pasteur, 33000 Bordeaux; secondaire: 120 av. du Maréchal de Lattre de Tassigny, Gujan-Mestras
named_cases_or_publications: Bauer, 'Le syndrome d'aliénation parentale reconnu par la Cour de cassation' (2013) — most-cited practitioner-camp commentary on Cass. 1re civ. 26 juin 2013 n° 12-14.392
entries.hook: Authored the single most-cited French practitioner-camp commentary on the 2013 Cassation arrêt.

entries.name: Caroline Mécary
entries.credentials: Maître; Avocate spécialiste en droit de la famille au Barreau de Paris; ex-membre Conseil de l'Ordre du Barreau de Paris
named_cases_or_publications: Counsel in Mairie de Bègles (first French same-sex marriage, 2004)
Pioneering FR jurisprudence on adoption by social parent post-PMA/GPA
11 books on droit de la famille / homoparentalité
entries.hook: Leading FR family-law avocate on non-traditional family structures; natural entry point for cross-cutting LGBTQ+-family / PA conversations.
entries.notes: Original brief named 'Christine Mécary' — corrected to Caroline Mécary, the prominent Paris family-law avocate with that surname.

entries.name: Ambroise Soreau
entries.credentials: Maître; Avocat au Barreau de Paris (admis 2007); doctorat en droit; CAPA EFB Paris 2001
named_cases_or_publications: Firm practice page on aliénation parentale framed as a gendered phenomenon disadvantaging fathers
Collaborations with SOS PAPA, PATERNET, CIRA fathers'-rights associations
entries.hook: Most visible FR avocat openly running an 'aliénation parentale' practice page — natural opposite-side interlocutor for any FR PA dossier.

entries.name: Cyril Braniste
entries.credentials: Maître; Avocat au Barreau de Paris (75001)
named_cases_or_publications: Braniste, 'Aliénation parentale, emprise : retour d'expérience, regard d'un avocat,' Village Justice (19 March 2021, updated 14 Jan 2025)
'Atteintes à l'autorité parentale, emprise, aliénation parentale, état de la Jurisprudence' — review of recent emblematic pleaded cases
entries.hook: Active recognition-camp Paris avocat with a 2021/2025-updated peer-review-style commentary on French PA jurisprudence.

entries.name: Asociación de Mujeres Juristas Themis
entries.credentials: Asociación / institutional feminist legal collective (abogadas, fiscales, juezas, letradas judiciales)

Themis 'Segundo Informe sobre Coordinación de Parentalidad' (2024)
Manifiesto jurídico feminista contra la Guía pro-SAP del CGPJ
entries.hook: Themis is the single best-organised Spanish jurists' collective on SAP / coordinación-parental critique — gateway to all named former-presidentas.

entries.name: Altamira Gonzalo Valgañón
entries.credentials: Abogada; ex-Presidenta Themis (2006–2010); Vicepresidenta Themis (2019–2021); Vocal CGPJ (nominated 2024)
named_cases_or_publications: Co-author Themis 'Tratamiento judicial' study (2024)
Co-author Themis 'Segundo Informe sobre Coordinación de Parentalidad' (2024)
Press spokesperson on SAP / coordinación parental
entries.hook: Themis vicepresidenta and current CGPJ vocal — highest-profile institutional Spanish jurist on SAP critique side.

entries.address: Avinguda de Jaume III, 11, 07012 Palma de Mallorca

Ex-presidenta Themis publications on SAP throughout 2000s
IBD institutional advocacy on PA / gender-violence policy
entries.hook: Bridges Themis legal collective and Balearic regional government on PA / gender-violence policy.

entries.name: Titti Carrano

entries.bar: Consiglio dell'Ordine degli Avvocati di Roma
named_cases_or_publications: Cass. civ. sez. I, ord. 24 marzo 2022, n. 9691 (caso Massaro) — leading advocate quoted on the ordinance's force
Sustained national advocacy via D.i.Re on PAS / alienazione genitoriale critique
entries.hook: Named legal voice on the most-cited Italian Cassation ordinance critical of PAS/alienazione genitoriale.

entries.name: Elena Biaggioni
entries.firm: D.i.Re — Donne in Rete contro la violenza

named_cases_or_publications: Author of D.i.Re practitioner document 'Ammonimento per violenza domestica'

Firm-site practice page on Cass. civ. I 9691/2022 and follow-on jurisprudence
entries.hook: One of the few Italian individual avvocati with a publicly indexed 'alienazione parentale' practice page from the critique camp.
entries.notes: Original brief named 'Avv. Marina Marino Roma' — that name resolves only to LinkedIn (Viale Mazzini 11) with no PA publications. Marconato substituted as the verified publishing avvocata on this exact topic.

entries.name: Geeske van Campen
named_cases_or_publications: Van Campen, 'Ouderverstoting,' Forward Advocaten blog (8 February 2021), citing Adviesrapport Expertteam Ouderverstoting / Complexe Omgangsproblematiek (Jan 2021) and Hoge Raad RFR 2021/26
entries.hook: One of the few named Dutch advocaten with a published blog post tying personal practice to the official Expertteam Ouderverstoting / Complexe Omgangsproblematiek 2021 report.

notes_on_expertteam.chair: familierechter Cees van Leuven (Rechtbank Zeeland-West-Brabant) — sitting judge, not practitioner; therefore not directory-eligible but the most-quoted Dutch authority on ouderverstoting
